story_arc: A quiet first-person passage through an almost empty computer lab becomes a small moment of reflection. Beginning with the stillness of unattended machines, the story shifts toward the window and the damp alley outside, turning an ordinary late-day campus or office moment into a private pause between work and leaving.
visual_rules: Keep the same building interior logic between lab and window space
Use grounded camera movement like walking, pausing, and looking
Favor natural room tone and subtle environmental detail over spectacle
Use continuity anchors from desks, blackboard, door, window frame, and wet alley
Avoid abrupt morphs or slideshow-style reframing
narration_text: I stayed later than I meant to. The lab had already gone quiet, screens dimmed, chairs pushed back like everyone had left in the middle of a thought. I lingered for a minute, listening to the hum of the room, then drifted to the window. Outside, the alley was wet and nearly empty, the buildings holding onto the last light. It felt like one of those small in-between moments when nothing happens, but something settles anyway.

shots.title: Quiet Survey of the Empty Lab
shots.camera: First-person static hold with a slight natural head-turn pan across desk rows, blackboard left, then toward the back door and one lit screen
shots.action: The viewer stands still near the front of the lab, taking in dim iMac rows, empty chairs, and the settled quiet of the room.
shots.transition_goal: Establish the interior layout, solitude, and first-person presence before any movement begins.
shots.voiceover_line: I stayed later than I meant to.
shots.prompt: First-person view inside a nearly empty computer lab at late day, standing still near the front of the room. Rows of iMac computers sit on dark desks, empty chairs are pushed back, a blackboard runs along the left wall, ceiling panel lights glow softly overhead, and a door with windows is visible at the back. Begin on a quiet hold, then make a slight natural head-turn pan from the desk rows toward the blackboard on the left, then ease back across the room toward the back door and a single lit screen. Keep the motion human and grounded, like a person pausing and looking around before leaving. Preserve realistic room tone and subtle environmental behavior: dim monitor glow, soft reflections on screens, still chairs, stable overhead lighting, no dramatic activity. Maintain the same interior layout and a calm, lingering atmosphere.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no teleporting camera, no impossible room changes, no exaggerated cinematic whip pans, no surreal lighting shifts, no flickering geometry

shots.title: Lingering on the Paused Workspace
shots.camera: First-person slow forward creep down the aisle between desks with a small glance left to the blackboard and back toward the lit workstation
shots.action: The camera eases deeper into the lab, passing pushed-back chairs and desk edges as if walking slowly through a room everyone just left.
shots.transition_goal: Shift from observation to gentle movement, preparing the path toward the window side of the room.
shots.voiceover_line: The lab had already gone quiet.
shots.prompt: Continue in the same first-person viewpoint and same lab interior, now easing into a slow forward walk down the aisle between desk rows. Pass close to desk edges and pushed-back chairs, with soft handheld sway and slight natural bob from careful footsteps. Briefly glance left toward the blackboard, then return attention to the one lit workstation deeper in the room. Keep continuity with the dark desks, iMac rows, ceiling panel lighting, and back door with windows. The room should feel recently vacated and quiet, as if work just ended. Let monitor reflections and the muted institutional atmosphere remain consistent while the camera advances at an unhurried pace. Motion should feel observational and reflective, not staged.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no jump cuts within the shot, no floating camera movement, no disappearing desks or chairs, no inconsistent lighting direction, no surreal motion blur

shots.title: Walking Toward the Window Side
shots.camera: First-person walking shot continuing through the room, following desk lines toward the brighter window area with mild handheld sway
shots.action: The viewer moves past the last workstations as reflections on screens and polished surfaces begin to give way to cooler light from the window.
shots.transition_goal: Bridge the lab interior to the window space without an abrupt change, keeping building logic continuous.
shots.voiceover_line: I lingered for a minute, listening to the hum of the room.
shots.prompt: First-person walking shot continuing deeper through the same computer lab, bridging naturally toward the window side of the room. Follow the lines of desks toward a brighter area ahead, moving past the last workstations with mild handheld sway and realistic footstep rhythm. Keep the blackboard, door placement, desk rows, and overhead interior light logically connected to earlier shots. As the camera progresses, reflections on computer screens and polished surfaces gradually give way to cooler window light, with the interior still faintly present in glassy surfaces. The building should remain coherent and grounded, shifting from workstation reflections to window reflections without any abrupt change. Preserve the quiet late-day mood and subtle environmental detail.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no sudden location swap, no impossible hallway expansion, no dramatic speed ramping, no chaotic handheld shake, no inconsistent architecture, no fantasy effects

shots.title: Settling at the Glass
shots.camera: First-person approach ending in a soft stop at the window, with interior reflections briefly visible in the glass before the view clears outward
shots.action: The camera comes up to the window and pauses, letting the room behind faintly reflect over the alley outside.
shots.transition_goal: Complete the move from workspace to reflection, allowing interior quiet to blend into the exterior view.
shots.voiceover_line: Then I drifted to the window.
shots.prompt: In continuous first-person motion, approach the window and come to a soft natural stop at the glass. Start with the lab still visible behind and around the frame, then let faint interior reflections of desks, screens, and overhead lights briefly overlay the outside view. As the camera settles, the view through the window becomes clearer: blinds and window frame edges are visible, and the damp alley beyond begins to dominate. Keep the movement subtle and human, like drifting up to the glass and pausing. Maintain continuity between the lab and the window space within the same building, with realistic reflection behavior and soft late-day light. The emotional tone is unwinding and private.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no mirror-like reflection glitches, no abrupt exposure jump, no impossible glass warping, no teleporting to exterior, no architectural discontinuity

shots.title: Looking Out Over the Wet Alley
shots.camera: First-person steady hold through the window frame with a subtle downward tilt to the rain-dark ground and yellow container, then a small rise to the buildings beyond
shots.action: The viewer studies the narrow alley, wet pavement, and neighboring office windows while remaining still at the glass.
shots.transition_goal: Deliver the reflective exterior payoff and contrast the enclosed lab with the open, damp city space.
shots.voiceover_line: Outside, the alley was wet and nearly empty.
shots.prompt: First-person steady hold at the window, framed by the window edges and partial blinds, looking out over a narrow wet alley. Begin with a subtle downward tilt to the rain-dark ground below and the yellow waste container, then slowly rise to take in the black grid-like office buildings beyond with a few lit windows. Keep the viewer stationary at the glass, with only a small natural breathing drift. The wet pavement should show subdued reflections, the alley should feel nearly empty, and the exterior light should be cool and late-day. Preserve faint hints of interior presence in the glass without losing the view outside. The contrast between the enclosed quiet lab and the damp open alley should feel calm and realistic.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no sudden weather changes, no moving crowds appearing from nowhere, no warped buildings, no unnatural rain effects, no overdramatic cinematic zooms

shots.title: Held Reflection Before Leaving
shots.camera: First-person locked-off hold at the window with almost imperceptible breathing motion, framed by blinds and window edges
shots.action: Nothing major moves; the gaze simply stays on the last light on the buildings and the quiet alley below.
shots.transition_goal: End on a calm, unresolved pause that feels like a private moment before turning away.
shots.voiceover_line: It felt like one of those small in-between moments when something settles anyway.
shots.prompt: First-person locked view at the same window, holding on the wet alley and the last light on the buildings across the way. Frame the shot with the window edges and blinds, keeping the yellow waste container and rain-dark ground below as continuity anchors. The camera remains almost still, with only imperceptible breathing motion and a faint sense of someone quietly standing there before turning away. Outside, nothing major happens: the alley stays subdued, distant windows remain softly lit, and the wet surfaces hold the evening light. Maintain realistic stillness, continuous building geography, and a calm unresolved mood that suggests a private pause at the end of the day.
shots.negative_prompt: no slideshow transitions, no abrupt morphs, no broken anatomy or distorted first-person body logic, no text overlays or captions, no freeze-frame behavior, no time-lapse sky changes, no sudden character appearances, no flickering lights, no warped perspective, no artificial dramatic action
